instruction: What do the different octane values of gasoline mean?
final_output:
The octane value of gasoline is a measure of its resistance to engine knocking or pinging. Engine knocking occurs when the fuel-air mixture in the engine ignites too quickly, before the spark plug has a chance to ignite it. This can cause damage to the engine over time.

Higher-octane gasoline is more resistant to engine knocking, while lower-octane gasoline is more prone to it.
